qas.q: During a contingency, what command and control functions should you understand?
qas.a: The crisis action team (CAT), emergency operations center (EOC), and unit control center (UCC)

qas.q: During a contingency, what center directs all base operability, survivability, and recovery operations?
qas.a: EOC

qas.q: Who directs the EOC?
qas.a: Support Group Commander

qas.q: Who commands and staffs CE's UCC?
qas.a: The chief of operations commands the UCC and the staff is predominately CE's senior enlisted

qas.q: Why do the aircraft runway and command and control facilities have a high priority for expedient repairs?
qas.a: Because they are critical to the base mission

qas.q: When CE's UCC is activated, where do the DATS assemble?
qas.a: In their staging areas

qas.q: Identify the career fields that ideally make up the DART
qas.a: One civil engineer officer (32E3), one 3E1 (HVAC/R), 3E4 (Utility), and 3E0 (Electrical). However, many bases use one member from 3E3 (Structural) and no officers

qas.q: What ADAT member is responsible for the team's movement when a UXO is involved?
qas.a: The EOD memeber

qas.q: The ADR teams are responsible for which repairs?
qas.a: Assessing damage and repairing the airfield, taxiways, airfield lighting, and arresting systems

qas.q: What center has the authority to determine what ADR repairs are needed and in what priority?
qas.a: EOC

qas.q: What should be your focus when shoring any weakened areas in a facility?
qas.a: To restore a minimum degree of structural integrity to the facility

qas.q: Guys are usually fashioned out of what items?
qas.a: Wire rope and turnbuckles

qas.q: How are guys normally installed?
qas.a: To function in opposing pairs to provide lateral restraint from such forces as wind or ice loading

qas.q: As a rule of thumb, when attaching wire rope clips for guys, what are the minimum requirements for 1/2- or 5/8-inch rope; 3/4- or 7/8-inch rope?
qas.a: Three clips spaced about 3 1/4" for 1/2" or 5/8" rope; four clips spaces 4 1/2" for 3/4" or 7/8" rope

qas.q: What is the use of jacking?
qas.a: To stabilize the structural integrity of a facility affected by vertical loads (the affects of gravity)

qas.q: When jacking with timbers, at what measurement is the timber cut?
qas.a: Half inch shorter than the distance from the top of top of the jack to the floor

qas.q: What does the sandwiching technique involve when splinting a damaged column?
qas.a: Sandwiching a damaged column in between two steel plates (splints) and secured with threaded rods

qas.q: Describe the use of stitching dogs to restore some structural integrity into a cracked concrete slab
qas.a: Take steel bars, normally rebar, and form it into a U-shape and place it over a crack like staples. The dogs should be of random length and variably spaced along the crack. The holes the dogs are inserted into must be well grouted to ensure a tight fit

qas.q: What life-threatening hazards can you be exposed to while on a damage assessment or repair team?
qas.a: Downed electrical wires that are live; ruptured gas lines with explosive vapors; chemical, biological, or nuclear agents; a UXO; or a structure that might be ready to collapse at any time

qas.q: What is the goal of expedient structural repair?
qas.a: To restore a facility to a safe condition with a degree of weather protection for occupants

qas.q: What type of fastener is used to attach a plywood patch over a concrete roof deck?
qas.a: Powder-actuated fasteners

qas.q: When making a metal roof repair, what action should you take to provide additional water protection on the upslope side?
qas.a: Place caulk or silicone on the upslope side

qas.q: When using the plastic sheathing protective technique to cover a hole in a wall, what size nailing strips should be used?
qas.a: At least 1x2" furring strips or 12-gauge, 1-inch-wide metal strips

qas.q: When patching holes in plywood or other nailable substrate (for walls), what size should you cut the nailing strips and plastic sheeting?
qas.a: Cut nailing strips 12 to 24 inches wider than the hole and cut the plastic sheeting 24 inches wider than the nailing strips

qas.q: When using plywood to repair a small hole in a nailable substrate (wall repair), how much overlap should you allow for over the undamaged siding?
qas.a: At least a 6" overlap

qas.q: When repairing a large hole in a load-bearing masonry wall, what size headers should you use to brace inside the hole?
qas.a: 2x8" double headers

qas.q: Which plan prepares us to react to unforeseen circumstances, such as an enemy attack, with an emphasis on restoring operations, saving lives, reducing human suffering, and minimizing further damage?
qas.a: Contingency response plan

qas.q: Which center is established as a command and control element to direct and monitor pre-attack survival actions and post-attack recover?
qas.a: Emergency Operations Center

qas.q: Who directs all base operability, survivability and recovery operations within the emergency operations center?
qas.a: Support group commander

qas.q: Which center does the damage assessment and response team report hazards?
qas.a: Unit control center

qas.q: What must the damage assessment and response team determine before entering a facility?
qas.a: Structural soundess

qas.q: Who controls the airfield damage assessment team?
qas.a: Emergency Operations Center

qas.q: Who normally oversees the minimum operating strip selection process?
qas.a: Base Civil Engineer

qas.q: Which center has the authority to determine which airfield damage repairs are needed and in what priority?
qas.a: Emergency Operations Center

qas.q: Which type of shoring is particularly effective when damage has been sustained to end or sidewalls yet roof members have been essentially untouched?
qas.a: Guys

qas.q: Which type of shoring is usually made of structural steel and heavy timbers?
qas.a: Braces

qas.q: They type of shoring used to stabilize the structural integrity of a facility affected by vertical loads (the effects of gravity) is
qas.a: Jacks

qas.q: Which shoring method is particularly useful with reinforced concrete columns that have not been seriously damaged, yet show signs of cracking or minor fracture?
qas.a: Splints

qas.q: Which shoring method restores some structural integrity into a cracked concrete slab?
qas.a: Tension ties (stitching dogs)

qas.q: When performing expedient repairs to a roof, what should your first efforts be towards?
qas.a: Ensuring adequate shoring and bracing

qas.q: How are emergency repairs made to fire-damaged trusses
qas.a: Scraping off the charred material and sistering a piece next to it

qas.q: After expedient patches have been made to a flat roof, the next action you must take is
qas.a: Ensure the roof still drains properly

qas.q: What is the first action you should take to repair a large hole in a load-bearing masonry wall?
qas.a: Shore and brace the area around the hole

qas.q: How are all of the base pieces played when assembling them?
qas.a: With the hooks on the outside of the shelter facing down

qas.q: When setting the arches onto the base frame, where should all of the bottom arches coded red be placed?
qas.a: On the same side of shelter

qas.q: In what sequence are the red and yellow coded purlins installed?
qas.a: You should begin installing the red-coded purlins first, and then work towards the opposite end where the yellow-coded purlins are installed

qas.q: What is used to secure the end panel base rope (at designated locations) to the base hooks on the outside of the base frame?
qas.a: The hook tool provided with the kit

qas.q: When working the main cover over the ends of shelter, how much overlap should you have?
qas.a: At least a four to five inch overlap.

qas.q: At what approximate location are guy anchors installed for the guy ropes?
qas.a: Approximately 36 inches away from the side of shelter

qas.q: Which linter is installed in the SSS first?
qas.a: The mid-liner

qas.q: When making minor repairs on the cover or floor, what size patch must you cut?
qas.a: Cut the patch large enough to extend a minimum of 3 inches on all sides of the damaged area

qas.q: What is used to make repairs for larger tents?
qas.a: A hand held heat welder and pressure roller

qas.q: What are the four single-headed spikes used for?
qas.a: To anchor the end base centers (two at each end)

qas.q: How is the floor installed on the MSS?
qas.a: Unfold the non-slip floor and slip the floor cutouts over the base stubs with the black side towards the ground

qas.q: How many sections are assembled together to form an arch assembly?
qas.a: Each arch assembly consists of six sections with one bottom arch (red coded) on each assembly

qas.q: When installing the purlins on the MSS, how are the pins placed?
qas.a: Place pins (installed from the top down) through the purlin sleeves and purlins.

qas.q: What is a ketter?
qas.a: The ketter is the edge of the panel that slides into a groove in the frame

qas.q: Once the top liner is in place, how do you check the frame for plumb?
qas.a: Hang a pullover rope from the top purlin. If the frame is plumb, the rope will hang straight down.

qas.q: What is used to apply tension to the main cover?
qas.a: The power pull and extension cable

qas.q: When is installing the duckbill anchors not required?
qas.a: When the shelter is anchored to a 4-inch reinforced concrete slab

qas.q: When installing the receptacles in the MSS, at what height above the floor are they placed?
qas.a: They should be 47 inches up from the floor

qas.q: In what position should the eight liner panels that come with the MSS be installed?
qas.a: They should be positioned with the center loop fasteners all facing towards the same end of the shelter

qas.q: Which shelter can be installed on concrete using thunder studs?
qas.a: The Dome Shelter

qas.q: Which shelter comes equipped with explosion proof lighting?
qas.a: LAMS

qas.q: The Aircraft Maintenance hangar has how many shipping containers installed in the corners?
qas.a: Four containers

qas.q: What distance must there be between latrines and water supplies and messing facilities?
qas.a: At least 100 yards

qas.q: What are the ground requirements when siting for a latrine?
qas.a: Construct the latrine on level ground (preferably) and never uphill from the campsite or water supplies.

qas.q: What size is recommended for a straddle trench latrine?
qas.a: 1 foot wide by 2 feet 6 inches deep by 4 feet long. There should be at least 2 feet in between trenches (if multiple trenches are located side-by-side) for footing.

qas.q: Within what distance of ground level should you close a straddle trench latrine?
qas.a: Within 1 foot of ground level

qas.q: What type of markings should be made to indicate there is a closed latrine?
qas.a: A sign "closed latrine" with the closing date placed firmly in the earth over the area

qas.q: What soil conditions require the construction of a separate urine soakage pit and what size should it be?
qas.a: When the latrine pit is in soil that absorbs liquids poorly; about 4 feet square by 4 feet deep.

qas.q: When assembling and installing the arch assemblies on the small shelter system (SSS) where are the red-coded arches placed?
qas.a: On the bottom; same side

qas.q: In order to secure the purlins to the arches on a small shelter system (SSS) slip the purlins through the
qas.a: Purlin sleeves and place pins from the top down

qas.q: When installing the main cover on a small shelter system (SSS), you should work the cover over the ends until you have at least a
qas.a: 4- to 5-inch overlap

qas.q: Approximately where and how are the guy anchors installed for the small shelter system (SSS)?
qas.a: 36 inches away from the side with the top of the anchor away from the shelter

qas.q: Identify the proper sequence for installing liners in the small shelter system (SSS)
qas.a: Mid liner, side liners, and end liners

qas.q: What is the first step when disassembling the small shelter system (SSS)?
qas.a: Ensure that all power is disconnected from the electrical system at the source

qas.q: When assembling a medium shelter system's (MSS) base frame, the four single-headed spikes are used to
qas.a: Anchor the end base centers

qas.q: When the cold weather kit is used how is the medium shelter system's (MSS) floor installed?
qas.a: Install the insulated floor pads first; then install the floor with the black-side down

qas.q: How many pullover ropes are used to install the top liner on the medium shelter system (MSS)?
qas.a: Four

qas.q: How many pullover ropes are used to install the main cover on the medium shelter system (MSS)?
qas.a: Six

qas.q: Which of the following procedures is not required when a medium shelter system (MSS) is anchored to a 4-inch reinforced concrete slab?
qas.a: Installing the duckbill anchors

qas.q: At which height above the floor are the receptacles installed in a medium shelter system (MSS)?
qas.a: 47 inches

qas.q: The shelter that can be installed directly on concrete using thunder studs is the
qas.a: Dome shelter

qas.q: Which shelter has a main structure that consists of 7 arches and two end walls
qas.a: Aircraft maintenance hangar

qas.q: The aircraft maintenance hangar (ACH) has how many shipping containers installed in the corners for office space?
You should construct field latrines on
qas.a: Level ground and never uphill from the campsite

qas.q: When digging multiple side-by-side trenches for the saddle trench latrine, how much space should you allow in between the trenches for footing?
qas.a: 2 feet

qas.q: What activities do engineer personnel provide after an air base attack?
qas.a: Damage assessment, MOS selection, explosive ordnance safing and disposal, bomb damage repair

qas.q: What does airfield damage repair assessment include?
qas.a: Evaluation of damage involving runway surfaces, taxiway surfaces, and other facilities that directly support aircraft operations

qas.q: What area(s) does the MAOS include?
qas.a: The MOS combined with access taxiways from aircraft staging areas such as shelters and parking aprons

qas.q: What is a crater? What are the characteristics of a large crater?
qas.a: Craters are damage that penetrates through the pavement surface into the underlying base and sub grade soil uplifting the surrounding pavement and ejecting soil, rock, and pavement debris around the impact area. Large craters have an apparent diameter equal to or greater than 15 feet

qas.q: List the three types of ADR repairs
qas.a: Expedient, sustainment, permanent repairs

qas.q: What does RQC provide?
qas.a: The purpose of the RQC is to provide a single number, expressed in inches, for each MOS crater repair

qas.q: What are the three main types of crater repair methods currently used in the Air Force?
qas.a: Debris backfill, choke ballast over debris, and sand-grid methods

qas.q: If crater repairs are not within RQC tolerance, who makes a decision whether to attempt aircraft operations under such conditions?
qas.a: The wing commander

qas.q: What are the height requirements for a filled-in crater in relation with adjacent undamaged pavement surfaces?
qas.a: It should be flush, but 3/4 inch, plus or minus, is allowed

qas.q: Why is AM-2 matting rarely used on the MOS?
qas.a: Because of the bounce that an aircraft encounters when its wheels hit the height difference between the patch and the undamaged pavement

qas.q: What are some of the precautions in repairing and/or laying AM-2 panels?
qas.a: It requires 200 to 300 percent more effort to place AM-2 than to remove it. The more exact the base course finished grade, the easier it is to lay AM-2. Six hours is about the maximum time a person can sustain production when handling AM-2. Use a sting line along the edge for proper alignment. To maintain proper alignment, always use a locking bar as a spacer behind the last joint. Never crowd the bundles of stacked panels next to the panels being laid.

qas.q: What is the recommended team configuration for AM-2 matting?
qas.a: A 16-man team consisting of: 1 mat chief to supervise construction; 1 alignment person to make sure that the first mat in each row is in proper alignment; 2 pry bar people to adjust the individual mats and to insert the locking bars; and 12 (six 2-man teams) mat installation people to lay the at in place

qas.q: Where is the assembled starter key lock placed?
qas.a: At the appropriate extended centerline of the crater (either parallel or perpendicular to the MOS centerline)

qas.q: When attaching towing tubes to the matting using the concurrent with key lock assembling method, what tubes are installed first and where are they installed?
qas.a: The two starter-towing tubers are placed on a 20-foot piece of assembled mandrel, centered on the special connector

qas.q: How are ramps properly aligned with the mat and what is used to fasten the ramps together?
qas.a: Use locking bar spacers between the mat and the ramp to keep it properly aligned; fasten ramps together with flat-head screws coated with anti-seize compound

qas.q: How thick is FFM? What is the size of a standard patch?
qas.a: About 3/8" thick. A standard patch consists of two panels when attached make the overall patch 60' x 54'

qas.q: What is used to join fiberglass mats?
qas.a: Joining panels that are installed with bushings and anchor bolts. One of each length (24' and 30') is needed to join two mats together

qas.q: When anchoring FFM to asphalt-overlaid concrete pavements, what size anchor bolts should be used?
qas.a: Normally 9 1/2" to 10 1/2" long anchor bolts that are 5/8" to 3/4" in diameter

qas.q: Describe how you re-anchor a FFM that was removed to fix a failed crater repair
qas.a: Remove all anchor bolts and bushings. Tow the.FFM away from the crater. Repair the crater. Check the crater to make sure it is within the tolerance for the FFM. Tow the FFM over the crater. Align the anchor bolt holes so that they are at least 6" away from the original drilled holes. Use the same anchoring system that was used to originally anchor the mat to the pavement.

qas.q: What is spall damage?
qas.a: Surface damage (missing pieces of pavement) that does not penetrate the base course and does not damage an area greater than 5' in diameter

qas.q: When repairing a spall, why should you use a jackhammer to form 1-inch-deep sidewalls when a spall is shallow and dish-shaped?
qas.a: This actin reduces the chance of the repair popping out when aircraft wheels repeatedly roll over the repair

qas.q: What is the preferred fill material used to repair spalls?
qas.a: Polymer concrete

qas.q: What determines the workability of magnesium phosphate cement?
qas.a: The air temperature

qas.q: How long should you mix magnesium phosphate cement ingredients before emptying it from the mixer?
qas.a: 1 to 2 minutes

qas.q: What is the main piece of equipment used by the MOS paint marking team?
qas.a: The paint-striping machine

qas.q: What materials are used to stripe the MOS?
qas.a: When striping the MOS, use a fast drying white traffic paint with reflective beads. Use black traffic paint to cover any existing markings.

qas.q: What three colors of traffic paint do you use to make contingency repairs to airfield pavements?
qas.a: Black, White, Yellow

qas.q: Which type of paint is preferred for improved night visibility?
qas.a: Reflectorized paint

qas.q: What does retroreflective mean?
qas.a: The light is reflected back to the source it came from

qas.q: Displaced threshold arrowheads and chevrons (which paint?)
qas.a: Retroreflective yellow

qas.q: Aircraft arresting system warning markings (which paint?)
qas.a: Retroreflective yellow

qas.q: Runway shoulder and closed runway markings (which paint?)
qas.a: Nonreflective yellow

qas.q: All primary taxiways and taxi-lane markings (which paint?)
qas.a: Retroreflective yellow

qas.q: All runway markings (which paint?)
qas.a: Retroreflective white

qas.q: Why is it important to have advanced planning and training in runway marking procedures?
qas.a: To make sure any losses from injury or death can be overcome

qas.q: How much paint must you procure and store for MOS markings and obliteration?
qas.a: Enough white paint for a 10,000 foot MOS and enough black paint to obliterate at least one half of all existing runway markings.

qas.q: Who directs the MOS marking teams to move the assembly area?
qas.a: The UCC

qas.q: What's the final task of the four-person MOS layout crew?
qas.a: The removal of any existing airfield marking signs

qas.q: Why do we place a 55-gallon drum of water on the paint striping machine trailer?
qas.a: To flush the paint machine system when the paint colors are changed

qas.q: How do you mark the centerline of the MOS?
qas.a: With a broken line with each section 50' long, 30"-36" wide, and spaced 50' apart

qas.q: What do you paint first after the MOS is identified?
qas.a: The end stripe (threshold or departure end as appropriate) at one of the MOS ends

qas.q: Why are existing runway markings obliterated with black paint?
qas.a: So they don't interfere with aircraft operations or mislead pilots

qas.q: List in order the priority areas for obliterating markings on the MOS.
qas.a:
(1) Threshold markings
(2) Designation markings
(3) Centerline markings
(4) Aircraft arresting system markings
(5) Taxiway lead in and/or lead out line markings
(6) Touch down zone markings
(7) Fixed distance markings

qas.q: What is hardening?
qas.a: The process of strengthening buildings and utility systems to resist the destructive effects of weapons or natural forces

qas.q: List the major categories of protective construction
qas.a: Hardened, protected, semi-hardened, collaterally protected, splinter protected, and exposure protected.

qas.q: What are some of the typical high priority facilities that receive hardening protection?
qas.a: Aircraft, command and control centers, personnel shelters, and communication centers.

qas.q: What is Concertainer revetments material made from?
qas.a: Galvanized wire panels covered with a geofabric liner

qas.q: When building multi-layer Concertainer structures, to what depth are the cells filled in the previous layer before installing the second layer?
qas.a: Approximately 3" from the top

qas.q: What is the purpose of capping the Concertainer revetments? Where are some of the methods?
qas.a: To prevent water entry into the fill material. Cover the revetments with tarpaulins or other roofing material, or cap with a 6" layer of concrete

qas.q: What components are most maintenance actions performed?
qas.a: Door tracks, door rollers (turret tanks, and guide roller), limit switches, deceleration valves, door drive assembly (hydraulic unit, electric motor and speed reducer)

qas.q: Where can you usually find repair methods for your specific aircraft shelter doors?
qas.a: In the manufacturer's instructions or TO

qas.q: What is the purpose of the deceleration valve?
qas.a: It slows down the speed of the door when it is within approximately 5' of the full closed position

qas.q: What turns off power to the door motor when it reaches the fully opened or closed positions?
qas.a: The limit switch

qas.q: Before performing any maintenance on HAS doors, what must be done?
qas.a: Turn the main power panel off and lookout and/or tag out the switch to prevent anyone from turning it back on

qas.q: Who determines and reports the location, types, and quantity of airfield pavement damage to the emergency operations center?
qas.a: Damage assessment team

qas.q: Which type of airfield pavement damage has an apparent diameter equal to or greater than 15' and penetrates through the pavement surface?
qas.a: Large crater

qas.q: Which type of airfield pavement repairs create an operationally capable minimum operating strip, based on projected mission aircraft requirements, in the fastest manner possible?
qas.a: Expedient

qas.q: Which airfield damage repair (ADR) set uses 91 persons and 61 equipment items?
qas.a: Small

qas.q: Which airfield damage repair (ADR) set uses 164 persons and 135 equipment items
qas.a: Medium

qas.q: What is the acceptable repair quality criterion that the filled-in crater should be in comparison with the adjacent undamaged pavement surfaces?
qas.a: Flush, plus or minus 3/4"

qas.q: How is the AM-2 matting starter key lock placed after it is assembled?
qas.a: Parallel or perpendicular to the extended crater centerline

qas.q: When attaching AM-2 matting towing tubes concurrent with the key lock assembly, how is the starter tube that receives the first piece of matting placed on the mandrel?
qas.a: With the prongs facing up

qas.q: To install the first row of AM-2 mats the correct procedure is to start with
qas.a: Full-size mats and lay left to right

qas.q: How do you keep the rows of AM-2 matting in proper alignment during assembly?
qas.a: Install locking bars in between the mats

qas.q: When assembling folded fiberglass mat (FFM), the mats are placed end-to-end about
qas.a: 4' apart

qas.q: When unfolding folded fiberglass mat (FFM), the criteria used on the side opposite the tow vehicle to lift each successive panel as the tow vehicle pulls the mat is at least a
qas.a: Four-member team or front-end loader with forks

qas.q: What action should you take to try to prevent the resin from flaking at the folded fiberglass mat (FFM) hinge locations?
qas.a: Have a vibratory roller make one pass down each hinge location and follow with a broom sweeper

qas.q: When using 9 1/2" long anchor bolts to anchor folded fiberglass mat (FFM) to asphalt pavements, what hole diameter and how deep should you drill?
When it is necessary to anchor folded fiberglass mat (FFM) that was moved to repair a failed crater repair, the mat positioned for Rancho ring by align the
qas.a: Anchor-bolt holes at least 6" from the original holes

qas.q: When repairing a spall with magnesium phosphate cement, you should mix the ingredients for
qas.a: 1-2 minutes

qas.q: Identify the type(s) of paint used for paint striping
qas.a: Nonreflectoriezed and reflectorized

qas.q: Which of the following paints is used for all runway markings
qas.a: Retroreflective white

qas.q: Which of the following paints is used on runway shoulder markings and closed runway markings?
qas.a: Nonreflective yellow

qas.q: Identify the paints used for all primary taxiway and taxi-lane markings
qas.a: Retroreflective yellow

qas.q: After the minimum operating strip (MOS) layout and marker placement is complete, what is the final task for the four-person team?
qas.a: Remove any existing airfield marking signs

qas.q: The paint striping machine's spray guns are adjusted to provide a
qas.a: 30" - 36" wide strips

qas.q: Where you encounter difficulty locating signs to identify taxiways, mark the identification letter on the pavement near the
qas.a: Centerline in 6' block letters

qas.q: Which type of hardening is only needed when the threat is great and time permits major military construction programming, design, and construction?
qas.a: Permanent

qas.q: You can connect additional sections of Concertainer together by
qas.a: Overlapping the coil hinges and inserting a connecting pin

qas.q: When constructing multi-layer Concertainer structures, approximately how high from the top should you fill the cells?
When removing a guide roller from a hardened aircraft shelter (HAS) door, you should open the door until the inside end of the door is within approximately
qas.a: 6' - 8' from the the speed reducer

qas.q: When removing a turret tank from a hardened aircraft shelter (HAS) door, you should place a 100-ton capacity jack near the
qas.a: Middle of the tank

qas.q: When removing a turret tank from a hardened aircraft shelter (HAS) door, how do you keep the weight of the door from rupturing the jack's hydraulic seals?
qas.a: Place a metal spacer underneath the door to hold the door off the tanks
